name: Cecebre - Encoro de Cecebre Return
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Cambre offer diverse landscapes, from tranquil reservoir paths to more challenging segments with elevation. The region is characterized by the Cecebre Reservoir and its surrounding Biosphere Reserve, providing peaceful routes through wetlands and forests. Cyclists can also explore the scenic Río Mero Fluvial Promenade and the varied terrain around Monte Xalo. This area provides a mix of paved and unpaved sections, catering to different cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Cambre The most popular touring cycling route is Cecebre - Encoro de Cecebre Return, a 9.5 miles (15.2 km) trail that takes about 1 hour to complete. This easy route offers views of the Cecebre Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Ponte Vella – Pedrido Bridge loop from Cecebre, a difficult 41.4 miles (66.6 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and varied terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the Encoro de Cecebre – Ribeiras do Mero - Barcés loop from Cecebre, a 64.2 miles (103.3 km) trail leading through the Cecebre Reservoir area and along the Río Mero, often completed in about 7 hours 42 minutes. Touring cycling around Cambre is defined by the tranquil Cecebre Reservoir, the scenic Río Mero Fluvial Promenade, and the varied terrain of Monte Xalo. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy, flat paths to more challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Cambre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.95 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cambre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Cambre?
A: Cambre offers a diverse network of over 20 touring cycling routes. These routes cater to various preferences, ranging from easy, mostly paved paths to more challenging segments with significant elevation gains and unpaved sections.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Cambre?
A: You can expect a varied terrain in Cambre. Routes often feature a mix of paved and unpaved sections, taking you through tranquil reservoir paths, along scenic river promenades like the Río Mero, and into more challenging areas around Monte Xalo. The region's diverse landscapes include forests, wetlands, and agricultural areas.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Cambre?
A: Yes, Cambre has several easy touring cycling routes suitable for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956583279/cecebre-encoro-de-cecebre-return" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cecebre - Encoro de Cecebre Return&lt;/a&gt;, which is 9.5 miles (15.2 km) long and offers pleasant views of the Cecebre Reservoir, making it an enjoyable ride for all ages.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Cambre?
A: Many of Cambre's touring cycling routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For instance, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3990905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Vella – Pedrido Bridge loop from Cecebre&lt;/a&gt; covers 41.4 miles (66.6 km) with significant elevation, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40341788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Cambre&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent circular option.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Cambre's routes offer access to several natural and historical highlights. You can cycle past the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5223936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cecebre Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, explore the surrounding Abegondo-Cecebre Biosphere Reserve known for birdwatching, or follow the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3901345" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mero River Trail&lt;/a&gt;. The ancient Church of Santa María de Cambre also serves as a picturesque starting point for some tours.

Q: Are there routes with significant elevation gain for a challenge?
A: Absolutely. For cyclists seeking a challenge, Cambre offers routes with substantial elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17332450" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Encoro de Cecebre – Ribeiras do Mero - Barcés loop from Cecebre&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is a difficult 64.2 miles (103.3 km) route with over 1400 meters of ascent, providing a demanding ride through varied terrain.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route in Cambre?
A: While specific parking areas for each route are not detailed, many routes start from central locations like Cecebre or Cambre itself. Given the region's commitment to cycling, you can often find suitable parking near popular starting points or attractions like the Cecebre Reservoir or the Río Mero Fluvial Promenade.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Cambre?
A: Cambre is enjoyable for touring cycling throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and less rainfall, making them ideal for exploring. Summer can also be good, though it might be warmer. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Cambre?
A: The komoot community highly rates touring cycling in Cambre, with an average score of 4.95 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the Cecebre Reservoir area, and the well-maintained paths along the Río Mero. The mix of easy and challenging routes also receives positive feedback.

Q: Are there any facilities like bike repair stations available in Cambre?
A: Yes, Cambre is cycling-friendly and provides amenities for cyclists. Bike repair and maintenance points have been installed in strategic locations, including along the Río Mero Fluvial Promenade and near the Cecebre dam, supported by local cycling clubs.

Q: Can I spot wildlife while cycling in Cambre?
A: The Abegondo-Cecebre Biosphere Reserve, which many routes traverse or pass by, is a protected area rich in biodiversity. It's an excellent spot for birdwatching, where you might see various species such as ducks, herons, waders, grebes, Osprey, and Black Kite, especially around the Cecebre Reservoir.

Q: Are there any routes that specifically explore the Cecebre Reservoir area?
A: Yes, the Cecebre Reservoir is a central feature of many touring cycling routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3932778" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cecebre Reservoir – Cecebre Reservoir loop from Cecebre&lt;/a&gt; is a great option that focuses on this tranquil environment, offering extensive views of the lake and its surroundings.
